The Suez Canal Authority has issued circular updating the coordinates for the Port Said North area for all vessels, effective from 1 November.
Circular No. (5/2025) states that the new coordinates for the area with the actual positioning of coordinates on Chart (M24) as follows:

hits.title: Suez Port - Wikipedia
hits.summary:
Coordinates: 29°57′0″N 32°33′0″E﻿ / ﻿29.95000°N 32.55000°E
The **Suez Port** (also called Port Tawfiq) is an Egyptian port located at the northern tip of the Gulf of Suez on the Red Sea at southern entrance of the Suez Canal, serving the canal and the city of Suez.

hits.title: Railway Ghost Towns and Sidings on the Nullarbor Plain
Lighthouses of Egypt: Mediterranean Sea - Ibiblio
hits.summary:
Located on the pierhead at Sallum, a Bedouin village about 8 km (5 mi) east of the Libyan border. Accessible by walking the pier. Site open, tower closed. Admiralty N6206; NGA 21428.

…

- ****[Ra's Alam el-Rum]****
    Date unknown. Active; focal plane 48 m (157 ft); white flash every 5 s. 10 m (33 ft) steel post, painted white with a red band, mounted on a concrete base. EAMS has a closeup photo and Google has a satellite view. Located on heights above a prominent cape about 15 km (9 mi) east of Mersa Matruh. Site open, tower closed. Admiralty N6194; NGA 21420.

…

EAMS has the closeup photo seen at right, Ayman Mohamed has a distant view from a nearby beach, and Google has a satellite view. This lighthouse was built to guide tankers arriving at the El Hamra Oil Terminal, just offshore, where oil from Egypt's Western Desert is shipped. Located on a promontory about 30 km (19 mi) northwest of El Alamein. Site status unknown. ARLHS EGY-044; Admiralty N6193; NGA 21418.

…

****Sidi Kerir North Breakwater****
    Date unknown. Active; focal plane about 14 m (46 ft); green flash every 2 s. Approx. 9 m (30 ft) white tower. No photo available but Google has a distant satellite view. The light guides vessels into the Sidi Kerir Oil Terminal about 30 km (19 mi) southwest of Alexandria.
Sidi Kerir is the western terminal of the Sumed Pipeline, which brings oil from Ain Sukhna on the Red Sea, bypassing the Suez Canal. Located on the north (main) breakwater of the harbor. Site and tower closed. Admiralty N6192.4.
- ****Sidi Kerir South Breakwater****
    Date unknown. Active; focal plane about 14 m (46 ft); red flash every 4 s. Approx. 9 m (30 ft) red tower. No photo available but Google has a distant satellite view. Located on the south side of the entrance to the harbor. Site and tower closed. Admiralty N6192.5.
- ****Sidi Kerir Range Rear****
    Date unknown. Active; focal plane about 35 m (115 ft); continuous red light. Approx. 31 m (100 ft) square skeletal tower. No photo available but Google has an indistinct satellite view. Located behind the beach on the east side of the harbor. Site and tower closed. Admiralty N6192.1.

…

Located on a spit at the westernmost entrance to the Dikheila containership harbor area in El Agami, about 20 km (13 mi) southwest of the center of Alexandria. Site status unknown. ARLHS EGY-012; Admiralty N6173.5; NGA 21412.5.
- ****El Dikheila (El Dikhelia) Breakwater****
    Date unknown. Active; focal plane 9 m (30 ft); four green flashes, in a 1+3 pattern, every 4 s. Approx. 6 m (20 ft) round tower painted red. Trabas has Arndt's photo and Google has a satellite view. Located at the end of the breakwater of the Dikheila harbor extending from the end of the El Ajami spit. Site status unknown. Admiralty N6191.2; NGA 21413.7.
- ****El Dikheila (El Dikhelia) Range Front****
    Date unknown. Active; focal plane 14 m (46 ft); white flash every 3 s. Approx. 13 m (43 ft) skeletal mast. Trabas has Arndt's photo and Google has a satellite view. Located at the end of a pier at the Dikheila harbor. Site status unknown. Admiralty N6191; NGA 21413.
  -

…

- ****El Dikheila (El Dikhelia) Range Rear****
    Date unknown. Active; focal plane 31 m (102 ft); white light, 1 s on, 1 s off. Approx. 27 m (89 ft) rectangular or triangular concrete tower painted with red and yellow horizontal bands. No photo available but Google has a satellite view. Located on the waterfront of the Dikheila harbor east of El Agami. Site status unknown. Admiralty N6191.1; NGA 21413.5.

…

****Great Pass (Great Pass Beacon) ****
    Date unknown (station established 1899). Active; focal plane 21 m (69 ft); white flash every 4 s. 21 m (69 ft) round cylindrical tower mounted on a round concrete pier. Lighthouse painted with red, green and white horizontal bands.
The tower has a lean due to a collision with a ship. Trabas has Arndt's 2022 photo and Google has a satellite view. This light marks a junction point on the main Great Pass channel into Alexandria; ships turn southward here to enter the El Dikheila harbor. Located about 1.6 km (1 mi) northeast of the harbor entrance. Accessible only by boat. Site open, tower closed. ARLHS EGY-046; Admiralty N6174; NGA 21370.

…

***** El-Boughaz el-Kebir (Great Pass Range Front) (2)****
    1908 (station established 1894). Active; focal plane 18 m (59 ft); three continuous lights, red near the top of the tower, white in the center, and red near the bottom. 20 m (66 ft) round tapered tower with a domed top rather than a lantern; the lights are shown through small openings.

…

Capt. Peter calls these lights "the most beautiful leading lights in the world." The range guides ships into the modern harbor of Alexandria, about 13 km (8 mi) southwest of the old city. The lighthouse stands beside the main street along the coast in an industrial area. Site appears open, tower closed. ARLHS EGY-013; Admiralty N6176; NGA 21356.

…

Upper half of tower painted black, lower half white. Hisham Abdel Ghani's 2018 photo is at right, Trabas has Capt. Peter's 2005 photo of the two range lights and Google has a satellite view. Located 740 m (0.46 mi) southeast of the front light, adjacent to a large petroleum tank farm. Site appears open, tower closed. ARLHS EGY-039; Admiralty N6176.1; NGA 21360.

…

The beacon was abandoned in 1908 when the line of approach was changed from 134° (southeast) to the present 113° (east southeast). Located on an islet just offshore, about 250 m (275 yd) northeast of the current front range light. Accessible only by boat. Site appears open, tower closed.

…

- ****Alexandria Quarantine Breakwater****
    Date unknown. Active; focal plane 20 m (66 ft); green flash every 3 s. 19 m (62 ft) square pyramidal skeletal tower, painted green. The top of the tower carries a rectangular slatted daymark painted green with a white vertical stripe. Trabas has Arndt's 2022 photo and Bing has an indistinct satellite view. Located at the end of the breakwater on the east side of the entrance to Alexandria's west harbor. Site status unknown. Admiralty N6182; NGA 21384.
- ****Arsenal Quay****
    Date unknown. Active; focal plane about 13 m (46 ft); green flash every 3 s. Approx. 12 m (39 ft) tapered square pyramidal skeletal tower, painted green with a white band at the top. Trabas has Arndt's 2022 photo and Google has an indistinct satellite view. Located at the end of a quay in Alexandria's west harbor. Site status unknown. Admiralty N6190; NGA 21412.

…

Located at the western tip of the Ra's el-Tin peninsula marking the entrance to the western harbor of Alexandria. Site probably closed (the lighthouse may be on an Egyptian naval base), tower closed. ARLHS EGY-017; Admiralty N6173; NGA 21344.

…

Trabas has Arndt's 2022 photo, Dmitry Lapin has a view from the sea, and Google has a satellite view. Located at the end of the breakwater on the east side of the entrance to Alexandria's west harbor. Site status unknown. Admiralty N6180; NGA 21380.

…

- ****Eastern Harbor West Breakwater****
    Date unknown. Active; focal plane 21 m (69 ft); green flash every 5 s. 10 m (33 ft) green post mounted atop a 1-story octagonal stone tower. Trabas has Arndt's photo, EAMS has a closeup, and Google has a satellite view. Located at the end of the breakwater extending eastward from the Qaitbay fort. Site status unknown. Admiralty N6171; NGA 21332.

…

- **- **Eastern Harbor Detached Breakwater****
  Date unknown. Active; focal plane 14 m (46 ft); red flash every 5 s. 6 m (20 ft) red post mounted atop a 1-story square stone tower. Trabas has Arndt's fuzzy photo, EAMS has a photo, and Google has a satellite view. Located at the west end of the detached breakwater opposite the west breakwater light. Accessible only by boat. Site status unknown. Admiralty N6170; NGA 21336.

…

This frequently-photographed lighthouse is on the "King's Tea Island" in front of the Montazah Palace at the eastern end of Alexandria. The palace was built by Khedive Abbas II around 1900 but the lighthouse was added by King Farouk I, apparently in the late 1930s. It does not appear that this lighthouse was ever an official aid to navigation but Maged Samaan has a 2008 photo showing it in action. Site open, tower closed.

…

****[Jazirat Disuqi (Nelson Island)]****
    Date unknown. Active; focal plane 22 m (72 ft); white flash every 5 s. 10 m (33 ft) steel post mounted on a concrete base. Upper half of the post painted red, lower half white. Trabas has Capt.

…

- ****#Abu Qir Range Front****
    Date unknown. Inactive since 2023 and apparently removed. This was originally a 20 m (66 ft) square skeletal tower originally mounted on a platform supported by piles; the location is now occupied by a large quay. Trabas has Capt. Peter's photo of the original light but the lighthouse is not seen in Google's satellite view. Formerly located about 100 m (110 yd) offshore on the south side of Abu Qir. Accessible only by boat. Site open, tower closed. ex-Admiralty N6168.3; NGA 21331.

…

****Maadiya (Mu'addīyah) Range Front****
  Date unknown. Active (privately maintained); focal plane about 19 m (62 ft); two continuous lights, white above red. 15 m (49 ft) square skeletal tower painted orange and carrying a triangular yellow daymark. Lucian Oniscu has a blurry street view across the harbor and Google has a satellite view.

…

Date unknown. Active (privately maintained); focal plane about 30 m (98 ft); two continuous lights, white above red. 24 m (79 ft) square skeletal tower painted orange and carrying a triangular yellow daymark. Ahmed Taha has a photo (lighthouse on the left) and Google has a satellite view. Located within the Petrojet facility 375 m (1/4 mi) south of the front light. Site and tower closed. Admiralty N6168.51.

…

Accessible by road. Site probably open, tower closed. ARLHS EGY-037; Admiralty N6166; NGA 21324.

…

This lighthouse and its sibling at Ra's Gharib on the Gulf of Suez (see the Egypt Red Sea page) are two of four lighthouses ordered from France by Ismail Pasha in connection with the opening of the Suez Canal. According to EAMS the Damietta lighthouse was "renewed" in 1992 and a new lighthouse was constructed at Borolus. Accessible by road. Site probably open, tower closed. ARLHS EGY-009.

…

EAMS has a photo, Hamid Abu-Zeid has a 2007 photo of both lighthouses, Ahmed HanOura has a photo showing both lighthouses, and Google has a satellite view. Located about 150 m (500 ft) southwest of the historic lighthouse. The light marks the extreme northern end of the Nile Delta at Cape Burullus, about 6 km (4 mi) north of Baltim and on the west side of the town of Burullus. Accessible by road. Site probably open, tower closed. Admiralty N6162; NGA 21320.

…

- ****Damietta (Dumyat) Port Range Front****
    Date unknown. Active; focal plane 33 m (108 ft); continuous red light. Approx. 30 m (98 ft) round steel tower. The lighthouse is left of center in a 2020 photo and Google has a satellite view. Damietta Port is an artificial harbor created as part of an industrial development on the west side of the Damietta Mouth. Located on the waterfront of the port. Site and tower closed. ARLHS EGY-033; Admiralty N6159; NGA 21317.
- ****Damietta (Dumyat) Port Range Rear****
    Date unknown. Active; focal plane 41 m (135 ft); continuous red light. Approx. 38 m (125 ft) square skeletal tower. The lighthouse is at the far left in a 2020 photo and Google has a satellite view. Located in the port area 170 m (185 yd) south of the waterfront. Site and tower closed. ARLHS EGY-033; Admiralty N6159.1; NGA 21317.1.

…

This lighthouse is part of the recent development of Damietta as an important port of entry. Located at the end of the west breakwater at the entrance to the Damietta Branch of the Nile. Accessible by walking the pier. Site open, tower closed. ARLHS EGY-014; Admiralty N6158; NGA 21312.

…

****Damietta East Breakwater (Ezbat el-Burj)****
    Date unknown. Active; focal plane about 12 m (39 ft); red light, characteristic unknown. Approx. 10 m (33 ft) round concrete tower with gallery, painted red. Faisal Al-Qallaf has a 2020 photo, Ahmed Elbestawy has a 2023 view from the sea, Wikimedia has Heba Otefy's photo of the light in action, Ibrahim Albadrawi has a photo showing both breakwater lights, and Google has a satellite view.
Located at the end of the east breakwater at the entrance to the Damietta Branch of the Nile. Site and tower closed. Oddly, this light is not listed by the Admiralty.

…

- **The French founded the city of Port Said (Bor Sa'id in Arabic) in 1859 at the northern terminus of the Suez Canal; they named it for the Egyptian ruler Muhammad Sa'id Pasha, who had granted them the right to build the waterway. Today it is Egypt's fifth largest city with a population of more than 600,000. Lights in this section are maintained by the Suez Canal Authority.**

…

**** * Port Said (3)****
    1997. Active; focal plane 47 m (154 ft); three white flashes every 15 s. 39 m (128 ft) square skeletal tower with lantern and gallery. The lantern is painted white, and the upper 2/5 of the tower carries a rectangular slatted daymark also painted white.
An EAMS photo is at right, Taha El Hossary has a 2019 photo, Omar Mido has a photo, and Google has a satellite view. The lighthouse is a sibling of the 1992 Borolus lighthouse seen above. Located about 2.5 km (1.5 mi) west of the entrance to the canal, across the street from the Port Said Stadium. Site and tower closed but the lighthouse is easily seen from anywhere in the neighborhood. Operator: Suez Canal Authority. Admiralty N5978.2; NGA 21276.

…

***** Port Sa'id (2)****
    1869 (station established 1860). Inactive since 1997(?). 59 m (194 ft) octagonal reinforced concrete, brick-faced tower with lantern and gallery, attached to 2-story keeper's house. The north (seaward) side of the tower is painted with a black and white checkerboard pattern; the rest of the tower is unpainted yellow brick; lantern painted white.

…

This lighthouse is the tallest lighthouse in Africa. Built to mark the northern entrance to the Suez Canal, it was designed by François Coignet based on the Phare des Baleines on the Île de Ré on France's Charente coast. It was one of the first large lighthouses to be built using the relatively new technology of reinforced concrete.
The checkerboard daymark pattern was applied in 1926. The first lighthouse was an octagonal wood skeletal tower. Located in downtown Port Said on the west side of the entrance to the canal. Site open, tower status unknown. ARLHS EGY-016; ex-Admiralty N5978.
